name: Peppered Beef Tenderloin with Horseradish Sau
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the spices and set aside.
2. Trim excess fat from the tenderloin and rub with the pepper mixture.
3. Place the tenderloin on a rack coated with cooking spray in a broiler pan.
4. Insert a meat thermometer into the thinnest portion of the meat.
5. Bake at 375°F for 50 minutes, or until the thermometer reads 140°F (rare) to 160°F (medium).
6. Transfer the tenderloin to a serving platter and cover with foil; let stand 10 minutes.
7. Slice the tenderloin and serve with Horseradish Sauce.
Horseradish Sauce:
1/4 cup low-fat cottage cheese
1/4 cup skim milk
3 tablespoons nonfat mayonnaise
1 1/2 tablespoons prepared horseradish
1 tablespoon lemon juice
1/8 teaspoon dried whole dillweed
Combine all ingredients in a container of an electric blender or food processor. Process until smooth and pour into a serving bowl.
Yield: 10 servings (approximately 197 calories per 3 oz tenderloin and 2 tablespoons sauce).
